mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of active periods, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion regime reflects strengthening supply-side capacity that boosts corporate output and drives positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during expansion regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive payroll growth months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction demand that drags on related sectors and generates negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where housing starts drop below the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with elevated policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and discount rates simultaneously, driving negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates sustained aggregate demand that supports earnings momentum and produces positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth signals lose predictive power or reverse direction in late-cycle regimes, with Rank IC dropping below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
